race.name: 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo
race.tagline: Coastal-start Marche hill country, no single climb but relentless rollers all day

vitals.date_specific: 2026: Sunday, September 20, 08:00 start. Medium route distance conflicts between official sources: course page says 87km/1,240m, regulations document still says 82km/1,220m — confirm final GPX before race day.
vitals.field_size: Gran Fondo entries capped at 1,500 (2026); ~1,600 riders started the two Gran Fondo routes in 2024
vitals.start_format: Mass start; Long (115km) and Medium (82-87km, sources conflict) route options

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 56. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 54.

course_description.character: Ten flat coastal kilometers out of Porto Recanati give way to relentless, scenic Marche hill country — there's no single alpine centerpiece here, and the difficulty instead comes from repeated short climbs, descents, and late-race ramps through Macerata, Filottrano, and Recanati.
course_description.signature_challenge: No single defining climb — the challenge is the accumulation: a 6.5km/4.5% opener at Porto Potenza Picena, rollers reaching 8%, Montecassiano/Montefano pitches around 5-7%, Filottrano's stepped 6-7% climb finishing at 8-9%, then more ramps near Montefiore and a late 2.5km/6% pitch before the final descent to Porto Recanati.
suffering_zones: Porto Potenza Picena climb — 6.5km at 4.5%, the opening test after the flat coastal start
Filottrano's stepped climb — 6-7% ramping to 8-9% near the top
Late-race pitches near Montefiore — roughly 2km at 6-7% and a final 2.5km at 6% before the descent home

logistics.airport: Ancona-Falconara (AOI), roughly 30-40 minutes from Porto Recanati
logistics.lodging_strategy: Porto Recanati and nearby Recanati/Macerata offer Adriatic-coast hotel options; book ahead for the September race weekend.

history.origin_story: Launched in 2021 as the Gran Fondo Vincenzo Nibali-5Mila Marche, organized by ASD Bike Division under former professional Andrea Tonti to promote Marche cycling tourism. The organizer calls 2026 the sixth edition.
history.reputation: A regional Gran Fondo World Tour stop with real organizer pedigree (Nibali branding, a former pro at the helm), though official 2026 documents still disagree with each other on the medium-route distance — a small but real gap in event execution worth knowing about.

biased_opinion.summary: 5 Mila Marche is a legitimate, currently-active Gran Fondo World Tour stop now in its sixth year, organized by ASD Bike Division under former professional Andrea Tonti and carrying Vincenzo Nibali branding from its 2021 launch. The course is honest rolling-hills racing rather than a single-climb spectacle — ten flat coastal kilometers into a string of short, punchy Marche climbs with no marquee summit. The one real paperwork flag: the official course page and the official regulations document disagree with each other on the medium route's distance (87km vs 82km) — worth confirming directly before race day rather than trusting either figure blindly.
strengths: Real organizer credibility — Gran Fondo World Tour stop, former pro (Andrea Tonti) running it, Nibali branding since the 2021 launch
Genuine, well-documented climbing content — five-plus named ramps and rollers with specific gradients, not vague scenery-first marketing copy
Two distance options (Long 115km, Medium ~82-87km) for different fitness levels
Solid field (~1,600 riders across both Gran Fondo routes in 2024; capped at 1,500 for 2026)
weaknesses: Official 2026 documents contradict each other on the medium route's distance/elevation (87km/1,240m course page vs 82km/1,220m regulations) — confirm the final GPX yourself before race day
Force-majeure cancellation policy offers no cash refund, only a following-year transfer minus a EUR10 fee
No single marquee climb — this is a rolling-hills accumulation race, which is a real strength for some riders and a letdown for anyone expecting an alpine-scale centerpiece
Regional event without the international prestige of Italy's headline gran fondos — a strong choice for Marche-based or nearby riders, less of a destination pull otherwise
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate regional Italian gran fondo built on relentless rolling hills rather than one big climb — a good pick if you're in the Marche region or want genuine, well-documented Italian hill-country racing without headline-event scale. Confirm the medium-route distance directly with the organizer given the conflicting official documents.

final_verdict.one_liner: A regional Italian gran fondo combining competitive cycling with cultural immersion through the Marche's rolling hills and Adriatic heritage.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Race it if you want genuine Marche hill-country climbing (five-plus named ramps, no single marquee summit) with real organizer credibility behind it. Confirm the medium route's exact distance directly with the organizer first, since the official course page and regulations disagree.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a bigger-name Italian gran fondo with similar rolling-hills character, look at Nove Colli.

videos.title: Siamo partiti già FINITI...? 😵 | Granfondo Nibali 5mila Marche
videos.description:
Ciao carissimi, il 16 e 17 Ottobre sono stato a Porto Recanati per la Granfondo Nibali 5Mila Marche.
Non ero in gara, non sono comparso in classifica, non che la cosa facesse una grande differenza visto il livello di allenamento.
Rappresentavo una delle nazionali social, un'idea degli organizzatori per coinvolgere più realtà social note nel ciclismo.

videos.title: Alla Scoperta della 5Mila Marche 2022
videos.description:
🔴Pronto ad Emozionarti? Metti il Casco, sali in sella e preparati a pedalare con Adriano Malori alla 5Mila Marche!
Un percorso unico ed una sfida emozionante attraverso i migliori paesaggi del paradiso del bike 🚴‍♂️

rider_intel.search_text: The only transcript available (tfYMGz03bLY) contains music lyrics, not rider commentary, and yields no actionable course intelligence. No rider-derived intel extracted; see race-data course_description and citations for organizer-sourced course details instead.

eligibility.notes: 6th edition, Sunday Sept 20 2026, Porto Recanati. Registration open via official site (links to ENDU portal). Official course page and regulations document disagree on the medium route's distance (87km/1,240m vs 82km/1,220m) as of this verification -- confirm final GPX directly with organizer.
